topics.title: Modal Verbs — Advice & Obligation
topics.explanation: "Should" gives advice. "Must" and "have to" show something is necessary or required.
topics.explanationAr: "should" للنصيحة. "must" و"have to" لشيء ضروري أو مطلوب.

examples.en: You should drink more water.
examples.ar: يجب أن تشرب المزيد من الماء (نصيحة).

examples.en: We must wear seatbelts in the car.
examples.ar: يجب أن نرتدي حزام الأمان في السيارة.

examples.en: I have to finish my homework tonight.
You shouldn't talk during the exam.
examples.ar: لا يجب أن تتحدث أثناء الامتحان.

topics.title: Passive Voice (Simple Introduction)
topics.explanation: We use the passive when the action is more important than who did it: "is/are + past participle".
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم المبني للمجهول عندما يكون الفعل أهم من الفاعل: "is/are + التصريف الثالث".

examples.en: The letter was written by Sara.
This bridge was built in 1990.
English is spoken in many countries.
examples.ar: تُتحدث الإنجليزية في العديد من البلدان.

examples.en: The cake was made by my mother.

topics.title: Past Continuous
topics.explanation: We use "was/were + verb-ing" for an action that was in progress at a specific moment in the past.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم "was/were + فعل-ing" لفعل كان قيد الحدوث في لحظة محددة في الماضي.

examples.en: I was sleeping at ten o'clock last night.
examples.ar: كنت نائمًا في الساعة العاشرة الليلة الماضية.

examples.en: They were playing football when it started to rain.
examples.ar: كانوا يلعبون كرة القدم عندما بدأت تمطر.

examples.en: What were you doing at that time?
examples.ar: ماذا كنت تفعل في ذلك الوقت؟

examples.en: She wasn't listening during the lesson.

topics.title: Past Continuous vs Past Simple
topics.explanation: Past continuous describes a longer action in progress; past simple describes a shorter action that interrupted it. We often use "when" or "while".
topics.explanationAr: الماضي المستمر يصف فعلاً طويلاً قيد الحدوث؛ الماضي البسيط يصف فعلاً قصيرًا قاطعه.

examples.en: I was watching TV when the phone rang.
examples.ar: كنت أشاهد التلفاز عندما رن الهاتف.

examples.en: While she was cooking, the fire alarm went off.
examples.ar: بينما كانت تطبخ، انطلق جرس إنذار الحريق.

examples.en: He broke his leg while he was skiing.
We were walking home when we saw the accident.
examples.ar: كنا نمشي إلى المنزل عندما رأينا الحادث.

topics.title: Gerunds & Infinitives
topics.explanation: Some verbs are followed by "-ing" (enjoy swimming), and others by "to + verb" (want to swim). These patterns need to be learned verb by verb.
topics.explanationAr: بعض الأفعال تتبعها "-ing"، وأخرى تتبعها "to + فعل". يجب تعلم هذه الأنماط فعلاً بفعل.

examples.en: I enjoy reading books before bed.
She wants to become a doctor.
We decided to leave early.
He finished doing his homework.

topics.title: As...As Comparisons
topics.explanation: We use "as + adjective + as" to say two things are equal, and "not as...as" to say they are not equal.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم "as + صفة + as" للتعبير عن التساوي، و"not as...as" لعدم التساوي.

examples.en: My brother is as tall as my father.
This test wasn't as difficult as I expected.
examples.ar: لم يكن هذا الاختبار صعبًا كما توقعت.

examples.en: She runs as fast as her older sister.
This city isn't as crowded as Cairo.
examples.ar: هذه المدينة ليست مزدحمة مثل القاهرة.

topics.title: Might / May (Possibility)
topics.explanation: We use "might" and "may" to talk about something that is possible but not certain.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم "might" و"may" للحديث عن شيء محتمل لكن غير مؤكد.

examples.en: It might rain later today.
She may come to the party tonight.
We might go to the beach this weekend.
examples.ar: قد نذهب إلى الشاطئ هذا الأسبوع.

examples.en: He may not know the answer.

topics.title: Reflexive Pronouns
topics.explanation: Words like myself, yourself, and himself are used when the subject and object of a sentence are the same person.
topics.explanationAr: كلمات مثل myself وyourself وhimself تُستخدم عندما يكون الفاعل والمفعول به نفس الشخص.

examples.en: I made this cake myself.
Be careful, you might hurt yourself.
She taught herself how to paint.
They enjoyed themselves at the party.
